message.content:
Update the existing statusline configuration at /home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh to add:

1. A **usage bar** - a visual progress bar showing API usage/cost percentage using block characters (▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█) that fills up as usage increases. Color it with the Naysayer palette: linefg (#126367) when low, warn orange (#ffaa00) when medium (50-79%), and red when high (80%+).

2. A **context bar** - a visual progress bar showing context window usage percentage using the same block character style. Same color coding as above.

Both bars should be compact (around 10 characters wide) and show the percentage next to them. Read the existing script first to understand the current format and integrate these bars naturally into the existing statusline layout.

content.text: Let me read the existing statusline script and settings first.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Claude Code status line — mirrors your p10k prompt
3	# Elements: user@host  cwd  git  time | model  context%
4	
5	input=$(cat)
6	
7	cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
8	model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
9	used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
10	
11	# user@host (linefg #126367)
12	user_host=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s@%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)" "$(hostname -s)")
13	
14	# cwd (blue #66D9EF) — abbreviate $HOME to ~
15	display_cwd="${cwd/#$HOME/\~}"
16	dir_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;102;217;239m%s\033[0m' "$display_cwd")
17	
18	# git info (linefg / warn on dirty)
19	git_part=""
20	if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
21	  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
22	  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
23	    # check dirty
24	    if ! git -C "$cwd" diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git -C "$cwd" diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
25	      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[38;2;255;170;0m*\033[0m' "$branch")
26	    else
27	      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$branch")
28	    fi
29	  fi
30	fi
31	
32	# time (fg #d0b892)
33	time_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;208;184;146m%s\033[0m' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)")
34	
35	# model (subtle)
36	model_part=""
37	[ -n "$model" ] && model_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$model")
38	
39	# context usage
40	ctx_part=""
41	if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
42	  used_int=$(printf '%.0f' "$used_pct")
43	  if [ "$used_int" -ge 80 ]; then
44	    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;255;0;0mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
45	  elif [ "$used_int" -ge 50 ]; then
46	    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;255;170;0mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
47	  else
48	    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
49	  fi
50	fi
51	
52	# Compose line
53	line="${user_host}  ${dir_part}${git_part}  ${time_part}"
54	[ -n "$model_part" ] && line="${line}  ${model_part}${ctx_part}"
55	
56	printf '%s\n' "$line"
57

content.text: Now I have a clear picture of the existing script. I'll add a shared `make_bar` function that renders the compact progress bar with the Naysayer color coding, then wire up two calls — one for API usage (rate limits) and one for context window usage — replacing the plain `ctx:N%` text with the new bars.

input.old_string:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Claude Code status line — mirrors your p10k prompt
# Elements: user@host  cwd  git  time | model  context%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')

# user@host (linefg #126367)
user_host=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s@%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)" "$(hostname -s)")

# cwd (blue #66D9EF) — abbreviate $HOME to ~
display_cwd="${cwd/#$HOME/\~}"
dir_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;102;217;239m%s\033[0m' "$display_cwd")

# git info (linefg / warn on dirty)
git_part=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
    # check dirty
    if ! git -C "$cwd" diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git -C "$cwd" diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[38;2;255;170;0m*\033[0m' "$branch")
    else
      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$branch")
    fi
  fi
fi

# time (fg #d0b892)
time_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;208;184;146m%s\033[0m' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)")

# model (subtle)
model_part=""
[ -n "$model" ] && model_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$model")

# context usage
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
  used_int=$(printf '%.0f' "$used_pct")
  if [ "$used_int" -ge 80 ]; then
    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;255;0;0mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
  elif [ "$used_int" -ge 50 ]; then
    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;255;170;0mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
  else
    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
  fi
fi

# Compose line
line="${user_host}  ${dir_part}${git_part}  ${time_part}"
[ -n "$model_part" ] && line="${line}  ${model_part}${ctx_part}"

printf '%s\n' "$line"
input.new_string:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Claude Code status line — mirrors your p10k prompt
# Elements: user@host  cwd  git  time | model  usage_bar  ctx_bar

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')
five_hour_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# make_bar PERCENT [WIDTH]
#   Renders a compact block-character progress bar with Naysayer color coding.
#   Color thresholds: <50% linefg #126367 | 50-79% warn #ffaa00 | 80%+ red
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
make_bar() {
  local pct="$1"
  local width="${2:-10}"
  local pct_int
  pct_int=$(printf '%.0f' "$pct")

  # Pick color based on threshold
  local r g b
  if [ "$pct_int" -ge 80 ]; then
    r=255; g=0;   b=0
  elif [ "$pct_int" -ge 50 ]; then
    r=255; g=170; b=0
  else
    r=18;  g=99;  b=103
  fi

  # Block characters in eighths: ▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█ (plus space for empty)
  local eighths_chars=' ▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█'

  # Total eighths filled = pct * width / 100 * 8
  local total_eighths=$(( pct_int * width * 8 / 100 ))
  local full_blocks=$(( total_eighths / 8 ))
  local remainder=$(( total_eighths % 8 ))

  local bar=""
  local i=0
  while [ $i -lt $full_blocks ]; do
    bar="${bar}█"
    i=$(( i + 1 ))
  done

  # Partial block (skip if remainder is 0 or bar is already full)
  if [ $remainder -gt 0 ] && [ $full_blocks -lt $width ]; then
    # Extract the nth character from eighths_chars (0-indexed, 0=space … 8=█)
    partial=$(printf '%s' "$eighths_chars" | cut -c$(( remainder + 1 )))
    bar="${bar}${partial}"
    full_blocks=$(( full_blocks + 1 ))
  fi

  # Pad with spaces to reach full width
  while [ $full_blocks -lt $width ]; do
    bar="${bar} "
    full_blocks=$(( full_blocks + 1 ))
  done

  # Wrap bar in color, append percentage label
  printf '\033[38;2;%d;%d;%dm[%s] %d%%\033[0m' "$r" "$g" "$b" "$bar" "$pct_int"
}

# user@host (linefg #126367)
user_host=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s@%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)" "$(hostname -s)")

# cwd (blue #66D9EF) — abbreviate $HOME to ~
display_cwd="${cwd/#$HOME/\~}"
dir_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;102;217;239m%s\033[0m' "$display_cwd")

# git info (linefg / warn on dirty)
git_part=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
    # check dirty
    if ! git -C "$cwd" diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git -C "$cwd" diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[38;2;255;170;0m*\033[0m' "$branch")
    else
      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$branch")
    fi
  fi
fi

# time (fg #d0b892)
time_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;208;184;146m%s\033[0m' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)")

# model (subtle)
model_part=""
[ -n "$model" ] && model_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$model")

# usage bar — 5-hour API rate limit (only shown when data is available)
usage_bar_part=""
if [ -n "$five_hour_pct" ]; then
  usage_bar_part=" $(make_bar "$five_hour_pct" 10)"
  # Prepend a dim label
  usage_bar_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103mapi:\033[0m%s' "$usage_bar_part")
fi

# context bar — context window usage (only shown when data is available)
ctx_bar_part=""
if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
  ctx_bar_part=" $(make_bar "$used_pct" 10)"
  ctx_bar_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103mctx:\033[0m%s' "$ctx_bar_part")
fi

# Compose line
line="${user_host}  ${dir_part}${git_part}  ${time_part}"
if [ -n "$model_part" ]; then
  line="${line}  ${model_part}"
  [ -n "$usage_bar_part" ] && line="${line}  ${usage_bar_part}"
  [ -n "$ctx_bar_part" ]   && line="${line}  ${ctx_bar_part}"
fi

printf '%s\n' "$line"

content.content: The file /home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here is a summary of what was changed in `/home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh`.

---

**What was added**

A `make_bar` function (lines 17-64) that:

- Takes a percentage and an optional width (default 10 characters).
- Computes fill using sub-character precision with the eight-step block sequence `▏▎▍▌▋▊▉█`, so the bar animates smoothly rather than jumping in full-block steps.
- Applies Naysayer palette colors automatically:
  - 0-49% — linefg `#126367`
  - 50-79% — warn orange `#ffaa00`
  - 80-100% — red `#ff0000`
- Renders as `[██████▌   ] 68%` (brackets + bar + space + percentage).

**Two new segments after the model name**

- `api:[bar] N%` — drawn from `.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage`; only appears after the first API response when Claude.ai subscription rate-limit data is available.
- `ctx:[bar] N%` — drawn from `.context_window.used_percentage`; only appears once at least one API call has been made in the session.

Both are omitted entirely when their data is absent (e.g., before the first message), so the status line stays clean at startup.

The `settings.json` at `/home/garrosh/.claude/settings.json` was not changed — the existing `"command": "bash /home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh"` entry already points to the right script.

---

For any further changes to the status line, use the **statusline-setup** agent — you can ask Claude to adjust colors, add new segments, reorder elements, or tweak bar widths at any time.
